assertion_results.text: Tasks target both 'trustify-backend' and 'trustify-ui' repositories with at least one task per repo
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks: task-1 (trustify-backend), task-2 (trustify-backend), task-3 (trustify-backend), task-4 (trustify-backend), task-9 (trustify-backend). Frontend tasks: task-5 (trustify-ui), task-6 (trustify-ui), task-7 (trustify-ui), task-8 (trustify-ui). Both repos have multiple tasks.

assertion_results.text: Backend tasks have lower task numbers than the frontend tasks that depend on them
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks are numbered 1-4 (create-branch, comparison-model, comparison-service, comparison-endpoint). Frontend tasks are numbered 5-8 (api-layer, comparison-page, sbom-list-selection, e2e-tests). Task 9 is the merge bookend. All backend implementation tasks (2-4) have lower numbers than frontend tasks (5-8).

assertion_results.text: UI-facing frontend tasks (pages, components) reference Figma design context mentioning specific PatternFly components and visual specifications — API-layer frontend tasks (API types, client functions, hooks) are exempt from this requirement
assertion_results.evidence: Task 6 (SbomComparePage) extensively references Figma design context with specific PatternFly components: Select (single, typeahead), Button (primary), Dropdown, ExpandableSection, Table (composable), Badge, EmptyState, Skeleton, SeverityBadge, CodeBranchIcon. It includes 'Figma design — PatternFly component mapping' section and 'Diff section specifications (from Figma)'. However, Task 7 (SbomListPage selection UI) is a UI-facing page task that modifies SbomListPage.tsx but does NOT reference Figma design context — it mentions PatternFly Table and Button but without Figma references. Task 5 (API layer) is correctly exempt. Since task 7 is UI-facing but lacks Figma design context, this assertion fails.

assertion_results.text: Cross-repo dependency ordering is enforced: either at least one frontend task explicitly depends on a backend task in its Dependencies section, or all tasks target a shared feature branch where backend tasks have lower task numbers than frontend tasks (implicit ordering via feature-branch workflow)
assertion_results.evidence: Task 5 (trustify-ui) explicitly depends on Task 4 (trustify-backend): 'Depends on: Task 4 — Add GET /api/v2/sbom/compare endpoint'. Additionally, all intermediate tasks target the shared feature branch TC-9003, and backend tasks (2-4) have lower numbers than frontend tasks (5-8). Both explicit cross-repo dependency and implicit feature-branch ordering are present.

assertion_results.text: Frontend tasks reference paths from repo-frontend.md; backend tasks reference paths from repo-backend.md
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks reference paths from repo-backend.md: task-2 references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs, summary.rs, details.rs; task-3 references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs, common/src/db/query.rs, common/src/error.rs; task-4 references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs, list.rs, get.rs. Frontend tasks reference paths from repo-frontend.md: task-5 references src/api/models.ts, src/api/rest.ts, src/hooks/; task-6 references src/routes.tsx, src/pages/, src/components/SeverityBadge.tsx, etc.; task-7 references src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx; task-8 references tests/e2e/, tests/setup.ts, tests/mocks/handlers.ts. All paths are consistent with their respective repo manifests.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 9 tasks have a '## Repository' section with a single repo name: tasks 1-4 and 9 have 'trustify-backend', tasks 5-8 have 'trustify-ui'. No task lists multiple repositories in its Repository section.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 9 tasks contain: '## Target Branch' (task-1: main, tasks 2-8: TC-9003, task-9: main), '## Description' (present in all), '## Acceptance Criteria' (present in all with checkbox items), '## Test Requirements' (present in all with checkbox items). Verified across all task files.

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the corresponding mock repository structure manifests (repo-backend.md or repo-frontend.md), not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: Backend Files to Modify: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs (exists in repo-backend.md), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs (exists), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs (exists). Backend Files to Create: comparison.rs in model/ dir (follows model/ pattern), compare.rs in endpoints/ dir (follows endpoints/ pattern). Frontend Files to Modify: src/api/models.ts (exists in repo-frontend.md), src/api/rest.ts (exists), src/routes.tsx (exists), src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx (exists). Frontend Files to Create: src/hooks/useSbomComparison.ts (in hooks/ dir), src/pages/SbomComparePage/ (follows pages/ pattern), tests/e2e/sbom-compare.spec.ts (in tests/e2e/ dir). All paths reference existing directories or files from the repo manifests.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every non-bookend task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance — bookend tasks (create-branch, merge-branch) are exempt as they omit Implementation Notes by design
assertion_results.evidence: Task 2: references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/summary.rs and details.rs for model patterns, specific derives (Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, Debug). Task 3: references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs with specific method signature, PackageService and AdvisoryService with paths, common/src/db/query.rs. Task 4: references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/list.rs and get.rs, common/src/error.rs::AppError. Task 5: references src/api/models.ts, src/api/rest.ts, src/hooks/useSboms.ts, src/hooks/useSbomById.ts, src/api/client.ts with specific function names. Task 6: references src/pages/SbomListPage/ and SbomDetailPage/, src/hooks/useSboms.ts, src/components/EmptyStateCard.tsx with specific PatternFly components. Task 7: references src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx, src/components/FilterToolbar.tsx. Task 8: references tests/e2e/sbom-list.spec.ts, tests/setup.ts, tests/mocks/handlers.ts. Bookend tasks 1 and 9 correctly omit Implementation Notes.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section — either all set to 'main' (direct-to-main workflow) or bookend tasks set to 'main' with intermediate tasks set to the feature issue ID (feature-branch workflow)
assertion_results.evidence: Feature-branch workflow is used. Bookend tasks: task-1 (create-branch) has Target Branch 'main', task-9 (merge-branch) has Target Branch 'main'. Intermediate tasks 2-8 all have Target Branch 'TC-9003' (the feature issue ID). This correctly follows the feature-branch workflow pattern.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: description-digest.md contains digests for all 9 tasks, each with sha256-md: prefix followed by exactly 64 lowercase hex characters. Examples: Task 1: sha256-md:d9fba1ee5a18bfbc0cdb055b4aac299b773f1d7f6e9ccf0cc59899b291de155c, Task 5: sha256-md:3990db2061a5104d7e566ddfe20644a61f9f8286e8e6596eea931bad0f292eb8. All 9 digests are real hex values (not placeholders). The file also documents the marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: <digest-value>'.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: No task contains convention enrichment in the prescribed format. No task references 'Per CONVENTIONS.md §' or uses the required rationale format 'Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'. Tasks mention 'conventions' informally (e.g., task-2: 'demonstrating the project's model conventions', task-4: 'per the error handling convention', task-5: 'naming and field conventions') but these are free-form prose references, not the prescribed format from convention-applicability-rules.md. No CONVENTIONS.md sections are formally referenced, no applicability rationales are provided.
